mechanism_narrative: Persistently high unemployment, as indicated by the unemployment rate, signals a weak labor market and subdued consumer demand, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration or contraction in retail sales indicates weakening consumer spending, which directly translates to lower corporate revenues and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate begins to rise from a low base while inflation remains elevated, it signals a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing price pressures, which increases borrowing costs and negatively impacts equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 0.25pp while CPIAUCSL 12-month growth is above 2%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1% and CPIAUCSL at 224.433 in 2016-10
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts during an economic expansion indicates robust demand and investment in the housing sector, which typically correlates with broader economic strength and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: housing market expansion with HOUST > 500k units
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances during housing market expansion, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window within this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals a significant slowdown in manufacturing and mining activity, indicating weakening economic fundamentals that lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the signal's predictive power is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in the Federal Funds Rate, even from low levels, indicates increasing borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, which acts as a headwind for economic growth and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of consistent job growth (rising PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (falling UNRATE) signifies a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which supports corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 100k and UNRATE 3-month average change < -0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131,028.0k and UNRATE at 9.0% in 2016-10
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Robust and accelerating real GDP growth provides a strong foundation for corporate profitability and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during periods of economic expansion.
regime_hint: economic expansion with GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances during economic expansion, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window within this regime.
